title: PHLORETIN
description: Phloretin is a natural dihydrochalcone flavonoid found primarily in apples and apple tree bark that exhibits anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties. It works by modulating multiple signaling pathways including NF-κB and Nrf2, trapping reactive dicarbonyl species, and inhibiting specific ion channels and glucose transporters. Research suggests potential benefits for cognitive function, inflammation reduction, and cellular protection against oxidative stress.

research.title: The Molecular Pharmacology of Phloretin: Anti-Inflammatory Mechanisms of Action
research.summary: Comprehensive review detailing phloretin's anti-inflammatory mechanisms through inhibition of cytokine signaling pathways, suppression of COX and iNOS, and protective effects in multiple organ systems including cardiovascular, hepatic, renal, and nervous tissue.

research.title: Nootropic, neuroprotective and neurotrophic effects of phloretin in scopolamine induced amnesia in mice
research.summary: In vitro and animal studies demonstrated that phloretin significantly influences neuronal membrane fluidity and potential, decreased excitotoxicity, and showed nootropic and neuroprotective effects in scopolamine-induced amnesia models.

research.title: Apple Polyphenols, Phloretin and Phloridzin: New Trapping Agents of Reactive Dicarbonyl Species
research.summary: Research identified phloretin as an effective trapping agent for reactive dicarbonyl species, providing antioxidant protection that may contribute to preventing oxidative stress-related diseases and cellular damage.

research.title: Effects of Phloretin and Phloridzin on Ca2+ Handling, the Action Potential, and Ion Currents in Rat Ventricular Myocytes
research.summary: Study revealed that phloretin affects cardiac cellular function by modulating calcium handling, inhibiting transient outward potassium currents, and increasing action potential duration in ventricular myocytes.

research.title: Flavonoids and their role in oxidative stress, inflammation, and human diseases
research.summary: Comprehensive review discussing how flavonoids including phloretin modulate oxidative stress and chronic inflammation, which are key drivers in pathogenesis of cancers, cardiovascular diseases, neurodegenerative disorders, and autoimmune conditions.

sentiment.summary: Very limited User feedback specifically about phloretin, with only one brief mention in a supplement stack context. Insufficient user experience data to establish clear sentiment, though related flavonoid compounds show generally positive but varied responses.

effects.label: Research Support:
effects.description: Strong preclinical evidence for anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects across multiple organ systems

effects.label: Bioavailability:
effects.description: As a metabolite of phlorizin (found in apples), bioavailability may vary depending on source and formulation

effects.label: Mechanism Diversity:
effects.description: Works through multiple complementary pathways rather than single-target action

effects.label: Clinical Data:
effects.description: Limited human clinical trials available; most evidence from in vitro and animal studies

dosage.label: Research Doses:
dosage.description: Studies typically use concentrations ranging from micromolar to millimolar in vitro

dosage.label: Transdermal Application:
dosage.description: One Reddit user mentioned transdermal phloretin use, though specific dosing not provided

dosage.label: Food Sources:
dosage.description: Can be obtained through apple consumption, particularly from apple peels and seeds

dosage.label: Standardization:
dosage.description: Limited commercial supplement standardization data available

sideEffects.label: Cardiac Effects:
sideEffects.description: May affect cardiac ion channels and action potential duration based on cellular studies

sideEffects.label: Limited Safety Data:
sideEffects.description: Insufficient long-term human safety data from clinical trials

sideEffects.label: Drug Interactions:
sideEffects.description: Potential interactions with medications affecting cardiac function or glucose transport

sideEffects.label: Individual Variation:
sideEffects.description: Effects may vary significantly based on individual physiology and concurrent medications

availability.label: Supplement Form:
availability.description: Available as isolated compound or in apple extract supplements, though less common than other flavonoids

availability.label: Natural Sources:
availability.description: Found in apples, apple cider, and apple tree bark products

availability.label: Research Chemical:
availability.description: More commonly available through specialty chemical suppliers for research purposes
